source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure IAM does not allow public access to Cloud Object Storage

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure IAM does not allow public access to Cloud Object Storage
input_row.description: IBM Cloud features the capability for users with specific access roles to create access policies that allow all users (authenticated and non-authenticated) to access resources in the account. This “all users” access in turn ends up in public (including non- authenticated) access to resources. Determine if this capability is required by your organization and disable if not required.
input_row.rationale: Some customer use cases require that certain files or data are made available for all users to access, both authenticated and non-authenticated. Even if the public access setting is enabled, resources in your account are not publicly accessible unless an administrator level user has explicitly created a policy to create public access for a resource. As a best practice, if this capability is not required by your organization, it must be disabled to prevent unintentional misuse. Impact: If the public access setting is enabled in the account, an administrator level user can create access policies to make resources publicly accessible. Resources are not publicly accessible unless an administrator creates an access policy, regardless of setting.
input_row.audit: Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access (IAM). 3. Click Settings. 4. In the public access section of IAM Settings, observe the Public access group setting. 5. If the Public access group setting is disabled, IAM is not providing public access to COS. 6. If the Public access group setting is enabled, proceed to the Access groups page by clicking Access Groups. 7. From the list of Access Groups, select Public Access by clicking on the Access Group name. 8. Ensure that there are no access policies present for COS in the list of access policies.
input_row.remediation: Console: To disable the Public Access Group: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access (IAM) . 3. Click Settings > Public Access . 4. Disable Public Access to disable the Public Access Group. To keep the Public Access Group enabled and verify that no access policies for COS exist: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access (IAM). 3. Click Settings. 4. In the public access section of IAM Settings, observe the Public access group setting. 5. If the Public access group setting is disabled, IAM is not providing public access to COS. 6. If the Public access group setting is enabled, proceed to the Access groups page by clicking Access Groups. 7. From the list of Access Groups, select Public Access by clicking on the Access Group name. 8. Ensure that there are no access policies present for COS in the list of access policies. 9. To delete an access policy, click on the action menu icon for the access policy and click Remove. API: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-public#disable-public-api Default Value: By default, the public access group is enabled and there is no public access allowed to COS. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-public
input_row.impact: If the public access setting is enabled in the account, an administrator level user can create access policies to make resources publicly accessible. Resources are not publicly accessible unless an administrator creates an access policy, regardless of setting.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control related to IAM public access to Cloud Object Storage (COS) can be verified using IBM Cloud APIs, IBM Cloud CLI, and IBM Cloud CLI. The control requires checking access settings for Cloud Object Storage, which is a standard IAM and storage service property in IBM Cloud, making it feasible for automated checking. The audit description indicates navigating through the IBM Cloud Console to check settings, suggesting that similar checks can be executed using IBM Cloud APIs or CLI commands. Additionally, since the control pertains to IAM and public access settings for resources, it aligns with automatable checks akin to IAM policies and S3-like configurations in other cloud platforms.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_iam_cos_public_access_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **API/CLI Methods:** Use IBM Cloud IAM APIs to check the status of the public access group settings and verify the presence of any access policies related to COS. APIs such as `iam.policy.list` can be used to review active policies.
- **CLI Commands:** Use IBM Cloud CLI commands to check public access groups and review policies for Cloud Object Storage. Commands might include `ibmcloud iam access-groups` to list and check access group configurations.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Focus on IAM settings to ensure that public access to COS is disabled either by turning off the public access group or ensuring no access policies grant such permissions.
- **Configuration Properties:** Check the status of the 'Public access group setting' and validate the absence of COS-related policies within it.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** Public access group for COS is disabled, or it is enabled but without any active policy allowing COS access.
  - **Fail:** Public access group for COS is enabled with active policies that grant access to Cloud Object Storage resources.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
